title: 10 best herbal creams for runny nose

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Echinacea, the key ingredient in purple coneflower cream, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can help alleviate congestion and excess mucus production.
Antiviral properties: The cream's antiviral compounds may help fight off viruses that cause respiratory infections, such as the common cold, which is often associated with a runny nose.

Mucilaginous texture: The creamy consistency of purple coneflower creams contains mucilages, which can help soothe and protect the delicate tissues in the nasal passages, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by excess mucus.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with runny nose because it contains compounds that help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea purpurea help to calm the nasal passages and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the cream's soothing effects can help to calm the skin and reduce irritation, providing relief from the discomfort associated with a runny nose. This natural remedy can provide quick relief and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, which can alleviate runny nose symptoms.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as chicoric acid and caffeic acid, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the underlying causes of runny nose, such as infection or irritation.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides, including cis-11-alkylamides and cis-8-alkylamides, possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the body's immune response and reduce excessive mucus production.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Echinacea purpurea flowers are commonly used to make creams for runny nose due to their high content of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Echinacea purpurea roots are used for their immunomodulatory properties, which help to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of runny nose symptoms.
Leaves: Echinacea purpurea leaves contain phenolic compounds, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried echinacea purpurea roots and leaves in the fall season.
Infuse 50 grams of the dried echinacea purpurea in 500 milliliters of cold-pressed oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax with 100 grams of shea butter and 50 milliliters of the infused oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal lemon balm cream helps to reduce histamine release and inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate congestion and running nose symptoms.

Vasodilatory effects: Lemon balm's vasodilating properties help to dilate blood vessels in the nose, allowing for easier drainage of mucus and reducing swelling and pressure, which can contribute to a runny nose.

Antimicrobial and antiviral properties: The antimicrobial and antiviral compounds present in lemon balm cream may help to combat infections that can cause runny nose symptoms, such as the common cold or sinusitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with runny nose because of its natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Melissa officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and borneol, have been shown to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure, thereby alleviating symptoms of a runny nose. Additionally, its calming and soothing effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can often exacerbate respiratory issues, making it a popular natural remedy for runny nose relief.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: It is a terpene that acts as a natural decongestant, reducing nasal congestion and runny nose by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators.

Rosmarinic acid: It is a phenolic compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate nasal congestion and runny nose by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages.

Oleanolic acid: It is a triterpenoid saponin that has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help relieve nasal congestion and runny nose by reducing inflammation and promoting the clearance of mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, including borneol, which helps to reduce nasal discharge and relieve congestion.
Flowers: They contain oils and compounds that have anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, making them useful for treating runny nose.
Stems: They also contain essential oils, including borneol, which helps to reduce nasal discharge and relieve congestion.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of melissa officinalis dried flowers in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to separate solids from liquids.
Combine the infused oil with 5g of beeswax and 5g of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the ingredients.
Add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and mix well to preserve the cream and prevent spoilage effectively.
Pour the warm cream mixture into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify completely.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Thymol, a key compound in thyme, reduces swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, which helps to alleviate congestion and runniness.
Antibacterial properties: Thyme's antibacterial compounds, such as carvacrol and thymol, combat bacterial infections that can cause excessive mucus production and rhinorrhea (runny nose).
Expectorant properties: Thyme's mucolytic agents help break down excess mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and runniness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with runny nose because it contains thymol, a natural decongestant that helps to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of thyme also help to combat infections and soothe the nasal passages, providing quick relief from runny nose symptoms. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties help to thin and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe and providing long-lasting relief from congestion. This makes thyme creams a popular natural remedy for runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial properties that help reduce nasal congestion and inflammation caused by bacterial or viral infections, thereby alleviating a runny nose.

Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene is a sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and reducing nasal discharge.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol is a phenolic compound with decongestant properties that help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate symptoms associated with a runny nose, such as nasal discharge and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of thymol, a compound known for its decongestant and antimicrobial properties.
Stems: They are used because they also contain thymol, which helps to relieve congestion and reduce the production of mucus.
Buds: They are used due to their high concentration of thymol, which makes them effective in treating respiratory issues like a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Extract the essential oil from 10g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves using a steam distillation process for 2 hours.
Combine 20g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter and 20g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture from step 2 in a double boiler at 160°f for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Add 10g of the essential oil extracted in step 1 to the mixture from step 3 and mix until well combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and rhinorrhea (runny nose).

Mucilages and demulcents: Herbal licorice creams contain mucilages and demulcents that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nose, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with a runny nose.
Expectorant properties: Licorice root has expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel excess mucus and relieve congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with runny nose because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe irritated nasal passages. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation and congestion, providing relief from a runny nose. Additionally, licorice creams may help to dry up excess mucus and reduce nasal secretions, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving nasal congestion and promoting a healthy respiratory system. It can be used as a standalone treatment or in conjunction with other natural remedies.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with runny nose by reducing inflammation in the nasal passages and alleviating symptoms of colds and respiratory infections due to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Licoricidin: It helps with runny nose by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical that causes blood vessels to swell and leads to nasal congestion and runny nose.

Licopyrones (Liquiritigenin and Licoricigenin): They help with runny nose by possessing anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help to regulate the immune response and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and congestion associated with a runny nose.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to produce glycyrrhizin, which is then used in creams to alleviate nasal congestion and runny nose.

Flowers: Although less commonly used, the flowers of Glycyrrhiza glabra may also contain some glycyrrhizin, although in smaller quantities, which can still contribute to their decongestant properties.
remedy_recipe: Mix 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder with 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Combine 100 milliliters of coconut oil with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a separate saucepan.
Stir the mixture of beeswax and glycyrrhiza glabra root powder for 5 minutes to melt completely.
Combine the melted beeswax mixture with the coconut oil and distilled water mixture in a blender.
Whip the blended mixture for 10 minutes to achieve a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Decongestant properties: Eucalyptus oil contains compounds like cineole, which helps to reduce nasal congestion by opening up airways and loosening mucus, making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Eucalyptus oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, sinuses, and throat, which can contribute to a runny nose.
Expectorant properties: The oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with runny nose because of its decongestant properties. The active compound eucalyptol in the plant has natural anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties that help to break down and clear excess mucus from the nasal passages. By reducing inflammation and congestion, Eucalyptus globulus creams provide quick relief from runny nose, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and flu. This helps to alleviate discomfort and promotes easier breathing.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (eucalyptol): This terpene helps to reduce nasal congestion and relieve a runny nose by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel, thereby providing quick relief.
Borneol: As a terpene, borneol has anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, which aid in reducing swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate a runny nose.

Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which help to open up airways and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part, as they contain high levels of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant that helps relieve nasal congestion and coughs.
Barks: The barks are also commonly used, as they contain a smaller amount of eucalyptol but still have decongestant properties and can help soothe sore throats.
Seeds: Eucalyptus seeds are less commonly used but are still utilized in creams due to their high eucalyptol content, which helps to thin mucus and relieve congestion.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 30g of eucalyptus globulus dried leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine 10g of the eucalyptus globulus powder with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over a double boiler at 160°f for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the bowl from the heat and stir in 20g of shea butter and 10g of vitamin e oil for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marigold cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.
Antibacterial: The cream's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause excessive mucus production and a runny nose.
Antioxidant: Marigold's antioxidant properties help soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with a runny nose.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with runny nose because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. Calendula's soothing and moisturizing effects also calm irritated tissues, promoting healing and relieving discomfort associated with a runny nose. This natural remedy can provide quick relief and support the body's natural ability to recover from respiratory issues. Regular use may also help alleviate recurring symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, which can contribute to runny nose.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids like caffeic acid and ferulic acid have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help alleviate symptoms of runny nose by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the nasal mucosa.

Flavonoids (specifically, rutin): Rutin, a flavonoid glycoside found in Calendula officinalis, has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that may help alleviate symptoms of runny nose, such as sneezing and congestion, by reducing inflammation and stabilizing mast cells.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm the nasal passages.
Leaves: They have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections in the nasal area.
Seeds: They contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that help to reduce swelling and combat the root causes of a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 flowers from the calendula officinalis plant and dry them in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Infuse 2 cups of dried flowers in 4 cups of jojoba oil in a glass jar for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks of infusion time.
Combine 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly until the beeswax and shea butter melt and the mixture reaches 120°f in 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lemon verbena's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause respiratory infections, while its anti-inflammatory effects soothe and reduce nasal congestion.

Decongestant and Antihistamine Effects: The cream's decongestant properties help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, while its antihistamine-like effects alleviate itchiness and sneezing associated with allergies and sinusitis.
Mucolytic Properties: Lemon verbena's mucolytic properties help break down excess mucus and phlegm, allowing for easier expulsion from the body and providing relief from a runny nose.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena officinalis, also known as lemon verbena, creams helps with runny nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The essential oils present in Verbena officinalis, such as limonene and linalool, have anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects that help to relieve nasal congestion and reduce excessive mucus production. These oils also have a soothing effect on the nasal passages, promoting easier breathing and providing temporary relief from runny nose and sinus pressure. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective solution for soothing a runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Verbascoside: Verbascoside is a phenylethanoid glycoside that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion in the nasal passages, thereby relieving runny nose.

Verbenaoside: Verbenaoside is another phenylethanoid glycoside found in Verbena officinalis, which has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the nasal mucosa, reducing the production of mucus and alleviating runny nose.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a phenolic compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, thereby relieving runny nose and other symptoms associated with respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain compounds with decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce nasal congestion.
Flowers: They have been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues, including runny nose, due to their expectorant and antiseptic properties.
Roots: They are rich in bioactive compounds that may help alleviate nasal congestion and other respiratory symptoms associated with runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried verbena officinalis flowers from a trusted source and dry them completely.
Steep the dried flowers in 100 milliliters of boiling water for 5 minutes to create a herbal infusion.
Strain the infusion and add 100 milliliters of distilled water and 20 grams of beeswax to it.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes to melt the beeswax completely.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Antihistamine activity: Ironwort creams contain flavonoids and saponins that have been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical mediator involved in allergic reactions, which can help reduce nasal congestion and rhinorrhea.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby relieving symptoms of runny nose, sinus pressure, and facial pain.

Antimicrobial properties: Some herbal ironwort creams contain antimicrobial agents that can help combat infections such as rhinitis, sinusitis, or other respiratory tract infections, which may contribute to a runny nose.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sideritis scardica, also known as ironwort, creams helps with runny nose because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce nasal congestion. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to have a decongestant effect on the nasal passages, helping to alleviate excessive mucus production and relieve symptoms of a runny nose. Additionally, Sideritis scardica creams may help to soothe and calm irritated nasal tissues, promoting a sense of relief and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate nasal congestion and reduce the production of excess mucus associated with a runny nose.

Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene, carnosic acid has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.
Bornied: This is a sesquiterpene which helps to reduce the inflammation and the nasal congestion associated with a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate congestion and reduce nasal discharge.
Flowers: The flowers are utilized for their expectorant and decongestant properties, which can help relieve nasal congestion and promote a runny nose to clear out mucus.
Roots: The roots are used because of their ability to act as a natural expectorant and decongestant, helping to clear out mucus and reduce nasal discharge.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of sideritis scardica flowers and leaves at dawn when they are dry and free from moisture.
Combine the harvested sideritis scardica with 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler to melt the wax.
Add 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of jojoba oil to the mixture and heat for 10 minutes.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of aloe vera gel to enhance the cream's properties.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of plantain cream helps to combat infections and kill bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause or exacerbate a runny nose.

Sotheing and Protective: Plantain cream's soothing and protective properties help to calm irritated nasal mucous membranes, reduce itching and discomfort, and create a barrier against further irritation or infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, creams helps with runny nose because of its anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as aucubin and allantoin, reduce swelling and congestion, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, plantain creams have natural expectorant properties, helping to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages. This makes it an effective remedy for relieving nasal congestion and runny nose, promoting a clearer and healthier respiratory system. Its natural composition also makes it an attractive alternative to traditional medications.
remedy_constituents: Apolypodol: It is a phenolic compound that may help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating runny nose symptoms.
Aucubin: This iridoid glycoside may have anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties that help break down and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing runny nose.

Plantaginin: This phenolic compound may have decongestant properties that help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate runny nose by increasing blood flow and reducing inflammation in the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated nasal passages.
Seeds: The seeds are a source of mucilage, which can be extracted to create a protective barrier and reduce inflammation in the nose.
Roots: The roots of Plantago major contain mucilage and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 plantago major leaves and flowers from a clean area on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested plantago major leaves and flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Steep 10 grams of dried plantago major in 200 milliliters of olive oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Mix 10 grams of the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-proof bowl.

remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which help to reduce swelling and ease sinus pressure, making it easier to breathe through the nose.
Expectorants: Marshmallow root also contains expectorant properties that help to loosen and thin out mucus, allowing for easy expulsion from the nasal passages, relieving congestion and runny nose.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with runny nose because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The root of the marshmallow plant is rich in mucilages, a type of complex carbohydrate that forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and congestion. This natural barrier helps to calm and protect the nasal passages, promoting a decrease in mucus production and a reduction in runny nose symptoms, providing relief and comfort to individuals suffering from nasal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion that can contribute to runny nose.

Phenolic acids (such as gallic acid and ellagic acid): Phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce swelling and infection in the nasal passages, alleviating symptoms of runny nose.

Saponins (such as althaea saponin): Saponins have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce nasal congestion and combat underlying infections that may be contributing to runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, a type of soluble fiber, which helps to soothe and moisturize the nasal passages.
Leaves: Contain mucilages that help to calm and protect the nasal mucosa, reducing inflammation and congestion.
Stems: Also contain mucilages, which can be extracted and used in creams to provide similar benefits to the roots and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 500 grams of althaea officinalis root powder and set aside for future use.
Combine 500 milliliters of distilled water and 50 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof container.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has fully dissolved in 30 minutes.
Add 100 grams of sweet almond oil and 50 grams of candelilla wax to the mixture and stir until melted in 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and allow to cool completely in 60 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the nasal passages. The cream's active ingredients, such as apigenin and luteolin, work to reduce swelling and ease congestion, allowing for a more normal flow of mucus. Additionally, chamomile's antihistamine-like effects help to combat excess histamine release, further reducing nasal discharge and providing relief from constant sniffles and congestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from runny nose and sinus pressure.

Antihistamine-like effects: The apigenin and luteolin flavonoids in chamomile have been shown to inhibit histamine release, reducing itching, sneezing, and runny nose associated with allergic reactions.
Soothing and calming properties: Chamomile's natural sedative properties help calm the mucous membranes, reducing nasal secretions and promoting a sense of relaxation, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce nasal congestion and soothe a runny nose.
Roots: Contains apigenin and other flavonoids that have antihistamine properties, which can help relieve allergy-related runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated ensuring optimal essential oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers using shade drying or low-temperature oven drying for at least 24 hours to preserve potency.
Combine one part dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with three parts coconut oil in a clean glass jar and infuse it for 2 weeks allowing gentle heat and light exposure.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids and discard any impurities within 24 hours.
Mix 10% of the resulting chamomilla oil with 90% beeswax in a double boiler heating to 180°f melting both ingredients together before whipping to desired consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing the excess mucus production that leads to a runny nose. Additionally, the cream's antihistamine-like effects can help alleviate sneezing and itching associated with allergies or colds. As a result, applying St John's wort creams topically can provide quick relief from nasal congestion and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort cream contains antioxidants like hypericin, which helps to neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, leading to reduced congestion and runniness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in St John's Wort cream have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and mucus production in the nasal tissues, providing relief from a runny nose.

Antiviral properties: St John's Wort has antiviral compounds that may help combat viral infections such as the common cold, which is often associated with a runny nose; these compounds can also reduce the severity of symptoms and shorten the duration of illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce nasal congestion and swelling.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and hyperforin, which have natural antihistamine and decongestant properties to alleviate runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers at peak bloom time for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested st john's wort flowers in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or using a dehydrator on low heat.
Grind dried st john's wort flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 30g of ground hypericum perforatum with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2-3 weeks in a glass jar.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard solids before pouring into sterilized containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as elderberry and chamomile, work together to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can help alleviate congestion and discharge. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with a runny nose. By providing relief from these symptoms, herbal elder creams offer a natural and gentle solution for managing a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Elder cream's antihistamine compounds help reduce histamine release, which can alleviate congestion and runny nose caused by allergies or colds.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder cream reduce swelling and redness in the nasal passages, which helps to ease congestion and sinus pressure, making it easier to breathe.
Expectorant properties: Elder cream's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose and allowing for easier drainage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and terpenes, which help to reduce nasal congestion and inflammation.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, making them effective in relieving runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered sambucus nigra with 20% glycerin and 80% coconut oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before stirring in 10ml of beeswax and 5 drops of tea tree essential oil.
Whip the mixture until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency using an electric whisk or spatula and store it in sterilized jars for up to 6 months.
Apply a thin layer of the cream topically to the nasal area twice daily, gently massaging it in with your fingers for 30 seconds before wiping off any excess with a clean cloth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with runny nose because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oil present in the cream has a soothing effect on the nasal passages, reducing congestion and swelling caused by allergies or colds. Lavender's decongestant properties help to thin out excess mucus, making it easier for the body to expel, providing relief from constant runny nose symptoms. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can also promote relaxation, which can help alleviate sinus pressure and discomfort associated with a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Lavender's antihistamine effects help to reduce congestion and alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, itching, and runny nose.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, like linalool, can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and reducing excess mucus production.

Natural decongestant properties: The essential oils present in lavender creams, such as eucalyptol, can help to clear out congestion and mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of essential oils, roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm irritated nasal passages.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, seeds provide a natural barrier against allergens and irritants, reducing nasal congestion and runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated, ensuring freshness and potency for infusion.
Combine 200g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container, allowing it to steep for 20-25 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids and retaining the clear liquid for use in cream production.
Melt 100g of sweet almond oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler set over simmering water, stirring occasionally until fully incorporated and reaching 180-190°f temperature.

Combine the infused lavandula angustifolia liquid with the melted sweet almond oil-beeswax mixture, pouring the blend into sterilized glass jars and allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as beta-carotene and lycopene, work together to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby relieving congestion. Additionally, damask rose has natural decongestant properties that help to shrink blood vessels, reducing excess mucus production and runny nose symptoms. By applying the cream topically, the soothing effects can be felt immediately, providing fast relief from a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The rose creams contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and excess mucus.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial compounds present in the cream help combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause a runny nose, promoting healing and reducing symptoms like discharge and postnasal drip.

Astringent property: The astringent properties of Damask Rose help constrict blood vessels in the nasal mucosa, which reduces swelling, congestion, and excess mucus production, providing quick relief from a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are effective in reducing nasal congestion and swelling associated with a runny nose.
Flowers: The fragrant and calming properties of damask rose flowers help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, providing relief from excessive mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of dried rosa damascena flowers and transfer them to a clean glass jar.
Combine 200ml of boiling water with the dried flowers and let it steep for 30 minutes under room temperature.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container discarding solids.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted, then slowly pour in the cooled infusion stirring constantly.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for at least 2 hours before transferring it to glass jars with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The cream's antioxidants also help to reduce swelling and congestion, allowing for easier breathing and a significant decrease in mucus production. Additionally, dandelion's natural antihistamine effects can help to alleviate symptoms of allergies, making it an effective remedy for seasonal sniffles and persistent runny noses.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, which help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby reducing runny nose.

Antihistamine properties: The flavonoids present in dandelion cream have antihistamine effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergic rhinitis such as excessive mucus production, congestion, and runny nose.

Mucilages and saponins: Dandelion cream contains mucilages and saponins, which have a soothing and protective effect on the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, helping to reduce irritation and inflammation that can contribute to a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated nasal passages.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and omega-3 fatty acids, which reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal cavity.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality taraxacum officinale herbs and filter them through a fine-mesh sieve to obtain the dried plant material.
Combine one part of the dried taraxacum officinale herbs with two parts of olive oil in a clean glass jar for infusing.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 48 hours, shaking the jar every 8 hours during the process, to extract the bioactive compounds.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids before mixing with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a 1:2 ratio.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the ingredients melt and emulsify to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cleavers creams helps with runny nose because it contains herbs that possess natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce nasal congestion and swelling. The cream's soothing ingredients also help to calm irritated mucous membranes, allowing for easier breathing and reduced discharge. Additionally, the antiseptic and antibacterial properties of cleavers work to combat sinus infections and other underlying causes of a runny nose, providing lasting relief from congestion and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cleavers contains compounds like flavonoids and triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce nasal congestion and swelling, thereby alleviating a runny nose.

Antihistamine properties: The plant's bioactive compounds, such as glycosides and phenolic acids, exhibit antihistaminic activities, which can help counteract the release of histamine in response to allergens or irritants, leading to reduced nasal secretions and congestion.
Mucolytic properties: Cleavers contains mucilages, which are natural demulcent agents that can break down and liquefy excess mucus, helping to clear out the nasal passages and relieve a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which help to soothe and calm irritated nasal passages.
Rhyzomes: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, they help to reduce swelling and congestion associated with a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh galium aparine leaves and flowers on a sunny day at peak bloom.
Dry the plant material in a warm, dry place for 7-10 days, stirring daily, until brittle.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking about 30 minutes to complete.
Mix 25g of the ground galium aparine with 75g of sweet almond oil in a glass jar, storing it in a cool place for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another jar, discarding the solids and transferring the infused cream to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram creams helps with runny nose because of its natural antihistamine properties, which soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The herb's essential oils, such as camphor and borneol, work to reduce inflammation and congestion, allowing for easier breathing and a decrease in excess mucus production. Additionally, marjoram's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out trapped mucus, providing rapid relief from a runny nose. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective way to combat seasonal allergies and colds.
remedy_properties: Decongestant property: Wild Marjoram cream helps to reduce nasal congestion by constricting blood vessels and reducing swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory property: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching in the nose, providing relief from discomfort and congestion.

Antimicrobial property: Wild Marjoram's antimicrobial agents work to prevent bacterial and viral infections that can cause runny nose, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to combat allergies and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh oregano leaves and dry them thoroughly in a warm place for at least four hours.
Combine one cup of dried oregano leaves with two cups of coconut oil and let it infuse for 24-48 hours.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar discarding solids.
Mix one tablespoon of beeswax with three tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler and heat until melted and smooth.
Gradually add the oregano-infused oil to the melted wax and butter mixture stirring constantly until fully incorporated and cooled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, help to reduce swelling and congestion in the sinuses, providing instant relief from constant sniffles and postnasal drip. Additionally, the cream's cooling sensation helps to constrict blood vessels, further reducing mucus production and promoting a clearer breathing experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle contains anti-inflammatory compounds like caffeic acid and quercetin, which help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating a runny nose.

Natural antihistamine properties: Nettle extract has natural antihistamine properties that help block the release of histamine, a chemical that causes blood vessels to swell and leads to increased mucus production, thus reducing a runny nose.
Expectorant properties: The mucilage present in stinging nettle cream helps loosen and clear out excess mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them an effective ingredient in calming and reducing nasal congestion.

Buds: Stinging nettle buds contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, making them an ideal component for medicinal creams to soothe a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves and dry them for at least two weeks in a warm dark place.
Combine one part dried nettle leaves with one part coconut oil and infuse overnight at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids after one hour.
Mix 20% beeswax and 80% infused nettle oil then heat gently until melted stirring occasionally for ten minutes.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers let cool at room temperature for at least four hours before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for runny nose are topical ointments infused with natural herbs that help alleviate nasal congestion and runny nose by reducing inflammation and promoting nasal decongestion. The best herbal creams for runny nose include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Melaleuca alternifolia, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Thymus vulgaris, Sambucus nigra, Rosmarinus officinalis, Thymus serpyllum, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, a runny nose can disrupt daily life, causing fatigue, difficulty sleeping, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting work, school, and personal relationships.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to runny nose that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, acne, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile help soothe and calm inflamed skin, reducing redness and itching. They also possess antibacterial and antifungal properties that combat infections and promote healing. These natural remedies offer a gentle and non-irritating alternative to harsh chemical-based treatments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with runny nose is a blend of Peppermint, Eucalyptus, and Calendula creams. Peppermint helps to cool and clear the nasal passages, while Eucalyptus oil's decongestant properties reduce congestion and promote drainage. Calendula cream soothes and calms the nasal tissues, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Together, these creams provide a powerful and natural solution to alleviate runny nose symptoms, promoting a healthy and balanced respiratory system.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by International journal of clinical pharmacology research, a novel polyherbal formulation (PF; Aller-7/NR-A2) comprising seven medicinal herbal extracts was used to treat 545 patients (321 males and 224 females) aged 18-59 years suffering from allergic rhinitis. The study, which involved a multicenter clinical trial over 12 weeks, used double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled studies in three centers and open-label studies in 11 centers. The results showed that the three major symptoms (sneezing, rhinorrhea, and nasal congestion) of allergic rhinitis were significantly reduced, making herbal creams a good option for relieving a runny nose caused by allergic rhinitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with runny nose because they contain eucalyptus oil, which has natural decongestant properties. When applied topically to the chest or nose area, the oil helps to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion. The cooling sensation of eucalyptus oil also helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, which provides relief from a runny nose. This can be especially helpful during cold and flu season, when a runny nose can be a persistent and uncomfortable symptom.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This compound helps to reduce nasal congestion and runny nose by acting as a natural expectorant, thinning mucus and facilitating its expulsion from the body.
Caryophyllene: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, which contributes to the relief of runny nose symptoms.
Limonene: This compound exhibits decongestant properties, helping to break down mucus and reduce its production, thereby alleviating runny nose and congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are commonly used to make creams for runny nose due to their high eucalyptol content, which has decongestant properties.
Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are used in creams for runny nose due to their mucolytic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to break down mucus and reduce nasal congestion.
Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are also used in creams for runny nose due to their expectorant properties, which help to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and store them in airtight containers for later use.
Combine 20g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 500ml of carrier oil in a double boiler setup.
Infuse the mixture for 2-3 hours or overnight to allow the eucalyptus globulus to release its properties.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the infused oil.
Blend the 100g of infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, work together to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion. This helps to stop excessive mucus production, thereby alleviating runny nose symptoms. By promoting a healthy balance of the body's natural defenses, Echinacea purpurea creams can provide long-lasting relief from runny nose and promote overall respiratory well-being.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, leading to a decrease in runny nose symptoms.
Flavonoids: Act as natural antihistamines, reducing histamine release and alleviating runny nose, sneezing, and congestion.
Alkylamides: Possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help decrease swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, contributing to relief from runny nose.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in bioactive compounds and are often used to create topical creams due to their anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Leaves: The leaves contain a significant amount of echinacoside, a key compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues.
Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are a primary source of alkylamides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate congestion and runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Extract echinacea purpurea flowers and roots to obtain a liquid extract containing 1:2 concentration of plant material.
Mix the extract with beeswax and shea butter in a 20% solution to achieve a smooth texture and consistency.
Combine the mixture with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a 1:1 ratio to enhance moisturizing properties.
Add a preservative like phenonip to the mixture at a concentration of 1% to prevent spoilage and contamination.
Pour the final mixture into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with runny nose because they contain the active ingredient tea tree oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. When applied topically, it can help soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion. The antimicrobial properties of tea tree oil also help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the runny nose, promoting a healthier respiratory system and faster recovery. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from runny nose symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This constituent has a decongestant effect, helping to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate runny nose symptoms.
Linalool: Linalool has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages.
Terpinen-4-ol: Terpinen-4-ol has antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, potentially alleviating runny nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of Melaleuca alternifolia's essential oil, which is used in creams for its antiseptic and decongestant properties.
Buds: Melaleuca alternifolia buds are rich in essential oils that are known for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them suitable for relieving runny nose symptoms.
Seeds: Although less common, Melaleuca alternifolia seeds are sometimes used for their antiseptic and decongestant properties, which can help alleviate runny nose issues.
remedy_recipe: Extract the essential oil from 20 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves using steam distillation for 2 hours.
Mix 1 gram of beeswax with 5 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of shea butter with 5 grams of aloe vera gel in a separate double boiler at 70 degrees celsius for 20 minutes.
Add 1 gram of the essential oil and 2 grams of vitamin e oil to the beeswax mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The active compound, gingerol, found in the creams, helps to reduce nasal congestion by thinning mucus and promoting its drainage. Additionally, the creams' warming effect on the skin helps to open up airways and ease sinus pressure, providing relief from runny nose and sinusitis. This natural remedy is a gentle and effective way to alleviate nasal congestion and promote a healthy respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate runny nose symptoms.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help combat infections and reduce nasal discharge.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve pain and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating runny nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, compounds known for their anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties.
Roots: Roots are also frequently used for their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion, making them a popular ingredient in over-the-counter cough and cold remedies.
Leaves: Leaves are sometimes used in combination with other parts, offering additional anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits that can help alleviate runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh zingiber officinale roots by digging them up with a shovel during late afternoon.
Grate 20g of the harvested roots using a food processor to release their active compounds quickly.
Mix the grated root with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler to create a smooth paste slowly.
Combine the paste with 100g of sweet almond oil and 20g of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl gently.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens and cools down to a creamy consistency within 15 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams help with runny nose because they contain glycyrrhizin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms the nasal passages. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to hydrate and protect the delicate tissues in the nose, reducing inflammation and congestion. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has natural antiviral and antibacterial properties that help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the runny nose. As a result, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams provide quick and effective relief from runny nose symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the nasal passages, thereby decreasing runny nose symptoms.
Flavonoids: As antioxidants, flavonoids can help prevent the production of excess mucus, which can contribute to a runny nose, by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Triterpenoids (specifically Glycyrrhetic acid): As a natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant, triterpenoids like Glycyrrhetic acid can help reduce nasal congestion and runny nose by shrinking swollen blood vessels and reducing mucus production.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that reduces inflammation and congestion.
Leaves: They are used due to their antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infections and soothe the nasal passages.
Roots: They are also used for their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 10g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 100ml of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to obtain the liquid extract.
Combine the extract with 20g of candelilla wax and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes to melt the waxes and mix with the extract.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams helps with runny nose because they contain thymol, a natural compound with decongestant properties. Thymol helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can cause congestion and excess mucus. By reducing inflammation and congestion, thymol in Thymus vulgaris creams can help to alleviate runny nose symptoms. Additionally, thymol's antibacterial properties can also help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the runny nose. This can provide relief and comfort to those suffering from a runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the underlying infection that may be contributing to the runny nose.
Caryophyllene oxide: Displays anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.
Bornyl acetate: Acts as a decongestant, helping to clear mucus and reduce nasal discharge.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are commonly used in creams due to their high content of thymol, a natural decongestant.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used in creams for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate runny nose symptoms.
Barks: Thymus vulgaris barks are sometimes used in creams for their expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the early morning.
Infuse 20 grams of the harvested plant material in 100 milliliters of cold-pressed oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter with the infused oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat and stir constantly until the beeswax and shea butter melt and thicken the cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams helps with runny nose because they contain active ingredients from the elderberry plant, which have natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream helps to reduce nasal congestion and soothe irritated tissues, thereby alleviating runny nose symptoms. The antiviral properties of Sambucus nigra also help to combat the underlying causes of a runny nose, such as colds and flu, by inhibiting the replication of viruses that cause these conditions. This natural remedy provides relief from runny nose discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help reduce nasal congestion and inflammation, alleviating the symptoms of a runny nose.

Phenylethylamine derivatives (e.g. tyramine and hydroxytyrosol): These compounds have vasoconstrictive properties, which can help reduce blood flow to the nasal passages, thereby decreasing mucus production and alleviating a runny nose.

Catechins: These flavonoids have antiviral properties, which can help combat the underlying viral infections that often cause a runny nose, such as the common cold, by inhibiting viral replication and reducing nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams due to their antiviral properties, which help combat cold and flu symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves are used in creams to provide anti-inflammatory benefits, reducing nasal congestion and runny nose.
Fruits (also known as Elderberries): Fruits are used in creams for their antiviral and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and protect the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of sambucus nigra flowers and leaves on a dry sunny day after the plant has fully bloomed.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for 2 weeks to preserve its medicinal properties.
Infuse 50g of dried sambucus nigra in 500ml of cold-pressed oil for 4 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer to a clean container.
Mix 10% of the infused oil with 90% of a base cream and stir well to create the final product.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with runny nose because they contain the essential oils of rosemary, which have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oils help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby decreasing the production of mucus and relieving congestion. Additionally, rosemary's antiseptic properties can also help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the runny nose. This natural remedy can provide quick and effective relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: Helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating symptoms of a runny nose.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the nasal mucosa, reducing congestion and runny nose.
Cineole (eucalyptol): Acts as a natural decongestant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, thus providing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the nasal passages.
Flowers: They are used for their antiseptic and decongestant properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion and inflammation.
Buds: They are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 200g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in 1 liter of cold-pressed carrier oil for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the infusion and discard the solids then transfer the oil to a clean glass container.
Combine the infused oil with 10% beeswax and 5% shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture to 160-180°f and stir until the wax and butter have fully melted and emulsified.
Pour the hot mixture into a mold and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus serpyllum creams helps with runny nose because they contain thymol, a natural decongestant derived from the herb thyme. The thymol in these creams has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, thus alleviating the congestion and runny nose associated with colds, allergies, or sinus infections. The cream's active ingredients also help to dry up excess mucus, providing quick relief from nasal congestion and promoting easier breathing. This makes Thymus serpyllum creams a popular natural remedy for runny nose.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as a natural decongestant, helping to reduce nasal congestion and runny nose.
Carvacrol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may contribute to runny nose, while also reducing inflammation in the nasal passages.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate nasal inflammation, reduce congestion, and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are used in creams for their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used in creams for their antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which help to combat infections and soothe the nasal passages.
Stems: Thyme stems are used in creams for their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of thymus serpyllum flowers and leaves on a sunny day when they are in full bloom.
Steam distill the harvested thymus serpyllum flowers and leaves for 2 hours to obtain essential oil.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Mix 5 grams of the steam-distilled thymus serpyllum essential oil with 50 grams of distilled water in a separate container.
Combine the essential oil mixture with the beeswax mixture and blend until a smooth, consistent cream is formed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with runny nose because of its ability to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams can help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, thus providing relief from runny nose. This natural remedy can also help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, promoting a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nasal congestion and swelling, alleviating runny nose symptoms.
Bilobalide: A neuroprotective constituent that helps to reduce inflammation and stabilize the nasal mucosa, leading to a decrease in runny nose and congestion.
Ginkgolides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help to reduce the release of chemical mediators that cause nasal congestion and runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Seeds: The seeds are used because they contain ginkgolides, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic properties that can help alleviate a runny nose.
Barks: The barks are used for their flavonoid content, which is thought to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of ginkgo biloba extract and 50 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler at 160°f to 180°f for 10 minutes.
Mix 50 grams of coconut oil and 25 grams of shea butter with the melted beeswax for 5 minutes.
Add 20 grams of vitamin e oil and the ginkgo biloba extract to the mixture and stir for 3 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a tin mold and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.
